issues.description: GET /api/spaces/[id]/admin/members still returns raw SpaceMember rows. The findMany uses include without a SpaceMember select, and line 41 serializes { members }, so SpaceMember scalar fields such as userId, guestSessionId, restrictedBy, restrictedReason, and restrictedUntil from prisma/schema.prisma:264-275 are still exposed by the same admin members resource that PATCH now tries to sanitize. The current client refreshes this GET after PATCH via src/components/dashboard/member-table.tsx:48 and src/app/dashboard/spaces/[id]/members/page.tsx:14-19, so the PATCH allowlist does not actually prevent those fields from reaching the client after an update.
issues.recommendation: Add an explicit select to the admin members GET and return only the fields the member table needs, e.g. id, role, restriction, displayName, createdAt, user { id, name, email, image }, and guestSession { id, nickname }. Do not return restrictedBy/restrictedReason/restrictedUntil/userId/guestSessionId unless a dedicated audited admin use case requires them.

issues.description: POST /api/spaces now allowlists the JSON body at lines 157-164, but the create call still uses include at lines 150-152, which causes Prisma to materialize all Space scalar fields from the newly created row, including inviteCode and accessSecret, before manually dropping them from the response. That does not leak in the current response, but it is weaker than the stated select/include invariant of fetching only necessary fields.
issues.recommendation: Change the create call to an explicit select for id, name, and template { key, name } so the sensitive Space fields are not fetched for this response path at all.
issues.deferRationale: Current response body is sanitized, so this is an invariant hardening gap rather than an observed client-visible leak.
